title: Equal COLA Act
plain_summary: This bill changes how the government gives yearly raises to federal workers who retired under the Federal Employees Retirement System. Right now, these retirees get smaller raises than what inflation actually is when prices go up a lot. The bill would make sure these retirees always get the full inflation increase, just like retirees in the older retirement system already do. This means their retirement payments would keep up better with rising costs of living.
last_action_text: ASSUMING FIRST SPONSORSHIP - Mr. Walkinshaw asked unanimous consent that he may hereafter be considered as the first sponsor of H.R. 491, a bill originally introduced by Representative Connolly, for t
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Equal COLA Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Increase in COLA for FERS Annuities
sections.text: Part A makes changes to how much the government increases retirement payments each year for federal workers who are in the Federal Employees Retirement System (a retirement plan for government workers). Starting each December first, these retirement payments will go up by the same amount that prices have gone up during the year. The government will measure this by looking at how much prices have changed compared to the same time period in the previous year when an increase was made. The increase will be rounded to the nearest one tenth of one percent. Part B explains that these new rules will apply to all cost of living increases made after this law is passed. It will also apply to all retirement payments under this system, whether those payments started before this law was passed, on the day it was passed, or after it was passed.
